item.title: PHFNet: A Parallel Hybrid Fusion Network with Linear Attention for Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Longteng Li
Liang Guo
Xinchen Li
Xinrui Jiang
Xuan Wang
Dan Jing
Kai Wei
Kai Jin
Mengdao Xing
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) is challenging due to weak targets and complex backgrounds, which undermine the performance of purely convolutional neural networks (CNNs). While recent hybrid CNN–Transformer architectures attempt to integrate local details with long-range context, their global perception tends to be incomplete and unstable, primarily owing to the prohibitive computational cost of full global modeling. To address this limitation, we propose PHFNet, a parallel hybrid fusion network for IRSTD that enables continuous local–global feature collaboration across all network stages. Specifically, PHFNet incorporates a CNN branch dedicated to extracting fine local details and preserving target structures, alongside a Transformer branch equipped with a novel Local-Compensated Linear Attention (LCLA). LCLA introduces adaptive local compensation into linear attention to preserve target-related local cues during global aggregation and suppress structured local interference, while structured spatial interaction further reduces the representational gap across the two branches. In addition, a Bidirectional Multi-scale Refinement Attention (BMRA) module is introduced to refine fused features through multi-scale contextual aggregation and spatial constraints, thereby enhancing representation stability and suppressing structured background interference. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that the proposed PHFNet consistently outperforms existing IRSTD methods. The code will be released at https://github.com/Llt201/PHFNet. Published: 2026-04-27T19:49:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.822 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Longteng Li; Liang Guo; Xinchen Li; Xinrui Jiang; Xuan Wang; Dan Jing; Kai Wei; Kai Jin; Mengdao Xing

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3687647

Relevance: 0.822 (must_read)

Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) is challenging due to weak targets and complex backgrounds, which undermine the performance of purely convolutional neural networks (CNNs). While recent hybrid CNN–Transformer architectures attempt to integrate local details with long-range context, their global perception tends to be incomplete and unstable, primarily owing to the prohibitive computational cost of full global modeling. To address this limitation, we propose PHFNet, a parallel hybrid fusion network for IRSTD that enables continuous local–global feature collaboration across all network stages. Specifically, PHFNet incorporates a CNN branch dedicated to extracting fine local details and preserving target structures, alongside a Transformer branch equipped with a novel Local-Compensated Linear Attention (LCLA). LCLA introduces adaptive local compensation into linear attention to preserve target-related local cues during global aggregation and suppress structured local interference, while structured spatial interaction further reduces the representational gap across the two branches. In addition, a Bidirectional Multi-scale Refinement Attention (BMRA) module is introduced to refine fused features through multi-scale contextual aggregation and spatial constraints, thereby enhancing representation stability and suppressing structured background interference. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that the proposed PHFNet consistently outperforms existing IRSTD methods. The code will be released at https://github.com/Llt201/PHFNet.

item.title: Infrared and Visible Ship Image Fusion Based on Adaptive Cross-modal Feature Interaction and Multi-scale Frequency-domain Transformation
item.dc:creator: Limin Guo
Yuwu Wang
Xiaohai Zhou
Yue Wu
Guifu Yang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Infrared and visible image fusion serves as a pivotal technique for all-weather ship surveillance in maritime security applications, which can compensate for the inherent deficiencies of single-modal imaging under low-illumination and complex sea-state conditions. Nevertheless, existing fusion algorithms confront three core bottlenecks in coastal ship scenarios: first, insufficient robust feature representation capability against complex sea-sky backgrounds and multi-scale ship targets; second, inefficient cross-modal feature interaction between infrared and visible modalities; third, weak scene adaptability arising from static weight allocation in multi-scale frequency-domain fusion. To address these issues, this paper proposes an infrared and visible ship image fusion algorithm (AMTFuse) based on adaptive cross-modal feature interaction and multi-scale frequency-domain transformation, and constructs the IVSHIP dataset dedicated to ship image fusion. AMTFuse adopts an end-to-end framework and consists of three core modules: firstly, a contrastive learning-based feature enhancement network (CLFE) is developed, which mitigates small-target feature missing and insufficient modal discriminability in complex maritime scenarios via multi-scale feature distillation paths, cross-modal contrastive learning, and enhanced spatial attention; secondly, an adaptive cross-modal feature interaction module (ACMF) is devised, which breaks through the limitations of static interaction and poor scene adaptability in existing cross-modal Transformers by virtue of modal-aware gating, multi-level feature alignment, and dynamic attention updating; finally, a multi-scale dynamic frequency-domain fusion module (MSDF) is established, which implements multi-scale frequency-domain decomposition and content-aware dynamic weight fusion at the deep feature level, remedying the defects of spatial-frequency decoupling and static weighting in conventional frequency-domain methods. Experimental resul... Published: 2026-04-27T19:49:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.814 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Limin Guo; Yuwu Wang; Xiaohai Zhou; Yue Wu; Guifu Yang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3687444

Relevance: 0.814 (must_read)

Infrared and visible image fusion serves as a pivotal technique for all-weather ship surveillance in maritime security applications, which can compensate for the inherent deficiencies of single-modal imaging under low-illumination and complex sea-state conditions. Nevertheless, existing fusion algorithms confront three core bottlenecks in coastal ship scenarios: first, insufficient robust feature representation capability against complex sea-sky backgrounds and multi-scale ship targets; second, inefficient cross-modal feature interaction between infrared and visible modalities; third, weak scene adaptability arising from static weight allocation in multi-scale frequency-domain fusion. To address these issues, this paper proposes an infrared and visible ship image fusion algorithm (AMTFuse) based on adaptive cross-modal feature interaction and multi-scale frequency-domain transformation, and constructs the IVSHIP dataset dedicated to ship image fusion. AMTFuse adopts an end-to-end framework and consists of three core modules: firstly, a contrastive learning-based feature enhancement network (CLFE) is developed, which mitigates small-target feature missing and insufficient modal discriminability in complex maritime scenarios via multi-scale feature distillation paths, cross-modal contrastive learning, and enhanced spatial attention; secondly, an adaptive cross-modal feature interaction module (ACMF) is devised, which breaks through the limitations of static interaction and poor scene adaptability in existing cross-modal Transformers by virtue of modal-aware gating, multi-level feature alignment, and dynamic attention updating; finally, a multi-scale dynamic frequency-domain fusion module (MSDF) is established, which implements multi-scale frequency-domain decomposition and content-aware dynamic weight fusion at the deep feature level, remedying the defects of spatial-frequency decoupling and static weighting in conventional frequency-domain methods. Experimental resul...

item.title: 遥感图像开放词汇感知进展
item.dc:creator: Li Kaiyu
Cao Xiangyong
Jiang Zixuan
Meng Deyu
item.prism:publicationName: Journal of Image and Graphics
item.description: 传统的遥感图像智能解译技术大多建立在封闭集假设之上，高度依赖海量的人工标注数据，且在推理阶段仅能识别训练集中预先定义的固定类别。面对真实地球观测场景中复杂多变的地表环境、尺度剧烈变化的目标以及长尾分布的罕见地物，传统范式泛化能力受限，难以满足高度动态的开放世界解译需求。近年来，得益于视觉—语言基础模型的快速发展，开放词汇感知技术应运而生。该技术通过跨模态语义对齐打破了传统离散标签的束缚，在零样本与少样本场景下展现出强大的泛化潜力。然而，遥感影像独特的俯视成像视角、复杂的拓扑关联以及多源异构的物理模态，致使自然图像领域的通用大模型在向遥感垂直领域迁移时面临显著的领域鸿沟。为此，本文系统梳理并总结了遥感图像开放词汇感知领域的最新研究进展。首先，从数据和方法两个维度，阐述了遥感视觉—语言预训练数据集的构建策略，以及预训练架构从基础域适配向异构数据感知与地理先验增强的演进脉络；其次，全面剖析了开放词汇感知在零样本场景分类、跨模态检索、图像分割、目标检测与定位、变化检测以及三维点云理解等关键下游任务中的应用范式；最后，深入探讨了当前该领域在高质量训练数据匮乏、细粒度评测基准缺失、多源异构模态深层对齐不足及模型可靠性等方面面临的核心挑战，并从多模态大语言模型驱动的生成式感知、全模态基础模型演进、时空因果推演及星地协同计算等方向对未来发展趋势进行了系统展望，以期为推动遥感智能解译迈向真实开放世界提供详实的理论参考。 Published: 2026-04-27T07:36:09+00:00 Venue: Journal of Image and Graphics Score: 0.812 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Li Kaiyu; Cao Xiangyong; Jiang Zixuan; Meng Deyu

Journal: Journal of Image and Graphics

DOI: 10.11834/jig.260163

Relevance: 0.812 (must_read)

传统的遥感图像智能解译技术大多建立在封闭集假设之上，高度依赖海量的人工标注数据，且在推理阶段仅能识别训练集中预先定义的固定类别。面对真实地球观测场景中复杂多变的地表环境、尺度剧烈变化的目标以及长尾分布的罕见地物，传统范式泛化能力受限，难以满足高度动态的开放世界解译需求。近年来，得益于视觉—语言基础模型的快速发展，开放词汇感知技术应运而生。该技术通过跨模态语义对齐打破了传统离散标签的束缚，在零样本与少样本场景下展现出强大的泛化潜力。然而，遥感影像独特的俯视成像视角、复杂的拓扑关联以及多源异构的物理模态，致使自然图像领域的通用大模型在向遥感垂直领域迁移时面临显著的领域鸿沟。为此，本文系统梳理并总结了遥感图像开放词汇感知领域的最新研究进展。首先，从数据和方法两个维度，阐述了遥感视觉—语言预训练数据集的构建策略，以及预训练架构从基础域适配向异构数据感知与地理先验增强的演进脉络；其次，全面剖析了开放词汇感知在零样本场景分类、跨模态检索、图像分割、目标检测与定位、变化检测以及三维点云理解等关键下游任务中的应用范式；最后，深入探讨了当前该领域在高质量训练数据匮乏、细粒度评测基准缺失、多源异构模态深层对齐不足及模型可靠性等方面面临的核心挑战，并从多模态大语言模型驱动的生成式感知、全模态基础模型演进、时空因果推演及星地协同计算等方向对未来发展趋势进行了系统展望，以期为推动遥感智能解译迈向真实开放世界提供详实的理论参考。

item.title: Rethinking feature representations preferred by fine-grained detection in remote sensing scenes: From a regularization perspective
item.dc:creator: Xueru Xu
Zhong Chen
Guoyou Wang
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Fine-grained object detection, an essential task in remote sensing image interpretation, is particularly challenging due to the extreme inter-class similarity. Existing fine-grained detectors, with prevalent approaches of local region refinement, accurate localization guidance, and multimodal signal supplementation, primarily focus on refining features within the foreground to improve detection accuracy. However, their effectiveness is noticeably compromised when the original data is insufficient. We reconsider the potential factors affecting fine-grained detection accuracy and argue that the detection network is prone to learning a few specific fine-grained patterns when trained on a limited data scale or low data diversity, leading to feature representations that struggle to generalize to unseen data. Motivated by this, we introduce a regularization perspective to construct a one-stage fine-grained detector, specifically by designing a noise regularization branch for the data space and a semantic regularization branch for the feature space. The noise regularization branch facilitates learning more generalized fine-grained patterns by performing background random blending and foreground random concealing operations to construct richer data patterns, while the semantic regularization branch contributes to learning discriminative features within foregrounds by leveraging semantic correlations inherent in fine-grained categories for semantic calibration. Two fine-grained datasets for airplane detection are employed to evaluate detection performance. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed fine-grained detector consistently outperforms existing detectors while maintaining competitive inference speed and model complexity, thereby confirming that the regularization perspective provides an effective and practical approach for constructing fine-grained detectors with robust detection performance. Published: 2026-04-28T06:54:09+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.806 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xueru Xu; Zhong Chen; Guoyou Wang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113857

Relevance: 0.806 (must_read)

Fine-grained object detection, an essential task in remote sensing image interpretation, is particularly challenging due to the extreme inter-class similarity. Existing fine-grained detectors, with prevalent approaches of local region refinement, accurate localization guidance, and multimodal signal supplementation, primarily focus on refining features within the foreground to improve detection accuracy. However, their effectiveness is noticeably compromised when the original data is insufficient. We reconsider the potential factors affecting fine-grained detection accuracy and argue that the detection network is prone to learning a few specific fine-grained patterns when trained on a limited data scale or low data diversity, leading to feature representations that struggle to generalize to unseen data. Motivated by this, we introduce a regularization perspective to construct a one-stage fine-grained detector, specifically by designing a noise regularization branch for the data space and a semantic regularization branch for the feature space. The noise regularization branch facilitates learning more generalized fine-grained patterns by performing background random blending and foreground random concealing operations to construct richer data patterns, while the semantic regularization branch contributes to learning discriminative features within foregrounds by leveraging semantic correlations inherent in fine-grained categories for semantic calibration. Two fine-grained datasets for airplane detection are employed to evaluate detection performance. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed fine-grained detector consistently outperforms existing detectors while maintaining competitive inference speed and model complexity, thereby confirming that the regularization perspective provides an effective and practical approach for constructing fine-grained detectors with robust detection performance.

item.title: OAADet: One-stage Anchor-free Arbitrary Oriented Object Detector via Center-ness Shift Correction
item.dc:creator: Chenlin Fu
Yingying Zhu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Arbitrary Oriented Object Detection (AOOD) has gained significant attention in the field of remote sensing, owing to its remarkable localization capabilities. Existing AOOD detectors primarily utilize anchor-free approaches to avoid presetting a large number of anchors. However, the center-ness introduced by anchor-free methods exhibits shifting across branches, indicating a discrepancy in localization decoding for the same anchor point in classification and regression tasks. This negatively affects the loss calculation and classification scores, thereby limiting the performance of the detector. In this work, we propose a simple and efficient one-stage anchor-free arbitrary oriented object detector named OAADet that aims to correct center-ness shift by designing a consistent learning strategy as well as constructing a new head structure. First, we propose the Localization Capabilities Consistency Learning (LCL) strategy to dynamically integrate the discrepancy in localization decoding directly into the network’s optimization goals. Moreover, we design a Feature Selection-Interaction Head (SI-Head) to promote interactions between information from two branches as well as mitigate the misalignment between the receptive field and OBBs. Experimental results on four widely-used remote sensing datasets DOTA, HRSC2016, SSDD+, and HRSID show that our approach outperforms existing models. Without bells and whistles, OAADet not only achieves a 79.18% mean Average Precision (mAP) on the DOTA dataset but also showcases the least model complexity at this mAP performance level. Notably, the model complexity experiments demonstrate that our method reduces the number of parameters by up to 58.6 MB and the complexity by up to 285 GFLOPs at similar mAP performance level. Published: 2026-04-27T19:49:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.805 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chenlin Fu; Yingying Zhu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3687959

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

Arbitrary Oriented Object Detection (AOOD) has gained significant attention in the field of remote sensing, owing to its remarkable localization capabilities. Existing AOOD detectors primarily utilize anchor-free approaches to avoid presetting a large number of anchors. However, the center-ness introduced by anchor-free methods exhibits shifting across branches, indicating a discrepancy in localization decoding for the same anchor point in classification and regression tasks. This negatively affects the loss calculation and classification scores, thereby limiting the performance of the detector. In this work, we propose a simple and efficient one-stage anchor-free arbitrary oriented object detector named OAADet that aims to correct center-ness shift by designing a consistent learning strategy as well as constructing a new head structure. First, we propose the Localization Capabilities Consistency Learning (LCL) strategy to dynamically integrate the discrepancy in localization decoding directly into the network’s optimization goals. Moreover, we design a Feature Selection-Interaction Head (SI-Head) to promote interactions between information from two branches as well as mitigate the misalignment between the receptive field and OBBs. Experimental results on four widely-used remote sensing datasets DOTA, HRSC2016, SSDD+, and HRSID show that our approach outperforms existing models. Without bells and whistles, OAADet not only achieves a 79.18% mean Average Precision (mAP) on the DOTA dataset but also showcases the least model complexity at this mAP performance level. Notably, the model complexity experiments demonstrate that our method reduces the number of parameters by up to 58.6 MB and the complexity by up to 285 GFLOPs at similar mAP performance level.

item.title: MKD-Net:A Multi-Kernel Downsampling Network for Adjacent and Small-Scale Ship Detection in SAR Images
item.dc:creator: Yuhu Qiao
Zhihui Xin
Guisheng Liao
Penghui Huang
Rui Wang
Jiahui Wu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR), as an important remote sensing technology, is playing an increasingly important role in marine monitoring. Nevertheless, in ship detection tasks of SAR images involving complex near-shore backgrounds, blurred edges of adjacent targets, and dense small ships, false alarms and missed detections remain difficult to avoid. To this end, this letter proposes a ship detection network, termed MKD-Net, which is based on a multi-kernel downsampling (MKD) module. This method integrates two key designs with low parameter complexity. The MKD transfers spatial information into the channel domain and extracts information using multi-kernel convolution, which can obtain features under different receptive fields while preserving fine-grained image details. The content-adaptive coordinate attention (CACA) introduces a dual-layer gating mechanism that consists of basic learnable weights and content-adaptive weights. Compared with other methods, the proposed method uses only 2.9M parameters. Furthermore, it can effectively detect adjacent targets in complex backgrounds, as well as dense small-scale targets. The proposed method achieves optimal detection performance. Published: 2026-04-27T19:52:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters Score: 0.804 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yuhu Qiao; Zhihui Xin; Guisheng Liao; Penghui Huang; Rui Wang; Jiahui Wu

Journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters

DOI: 10.1109/lgrs.2026.3687720

Relevance: 0.804 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar (SAR), as an important remote sensing technology, is playing an increasingly important role in marine monitoring. Nevertheless, in ship detection tasks of SAR images involving complex near-shore backgrounds, blurred edges of adjacent targets, and dense small ships, false alarms and missed detections remain difficult to avoid. To this end, this letter proposes a ship detection network, termed MKD-Net, which is based on a multi-kernel downsampling (MKD) module. This method integrates two key designs with low parameter complexity. The MKD transfers spatial information into the channel domain and extracts information using multi-kernel convolution, which can obtain features under different receptive fields while preserving fine-grained image details. The content-adaptive coordinate attention (CACA) introduces a dual-layer gating mechanism that consists of basic learnable weights and content-adaptive weights. Compared with other methods, the proposed method uses only 2.9M parameters. Furthermore, it can effectively detect adjacent targets in complex backgrounds, as well as dense small-scale targets. The proposed method achieves optimal detection performance.

item.title: Rethinking Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation With Vision Foundation Models
item.dc:creator: Muxin Liao
Yingqiong Peng
Yuting Sun
Wenju Huang
Yinglong Wang
Yuhang Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
item.description: Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation (UDASS) studies the challenge of transferring knowledge from labeled source domains to unlabeled target domains. Existing UDASS methods rely on ImageNet-pretrained Transformer networks, but both the limited scale of ImageNet and the restricted capacity of the pretrained models constrain generalization, ultimately hindering performance. Recently, Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) pre-trained on large-scale datasets have achieved a significant generalization ability on downstream tasks, yet their potential for UDASS remains largely unexplored. Thus, in this work, we rethink and explore the potential of VFMs in UDASS and propose a framework via VFMs for UDASS. Specifically, we first construct a general VFM-based UDASS framework by combining different VFMs with various decoders, and then evaluate its effectiveness on a standard UDASS benchmark under both full-parameter and frozen-parameter fine-tuning paradigms. Then, we propose a generalized parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach containing a Dual Distribution Alignment (DDA) strategy and a Selective Feature Refinement (SFR) strategy. The DDA strategy aligns covariance differences while enforcing semantic consistency, thereby improving source–target distribution alignment. The SFR strategy employs learnable tokens to refine frozen VFM features based on the source–pseudo-target covariance, preserving domain-invariant information while adapting to target-specific cues. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in multiple UDASS benchmarks. The code is released on https://github.com/seabearlmx/VFM4UDASS. Published: 2026-04-27T19:50:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Muxin Liao; Yingqiong Peng; Yuting Sun; Wenju Huang; Yinglong Wang; Yuhang Zhang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3687760

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation (UDASS) studies the challenge of transferring knowledge from labeled source domains to unlabeled target domains. Existing UDASS methods rely on ImageNet-pretrained Transformer networks, but both the limited scale of ImageNet and the restricted capacity of the pretrained models constrain generalization, ultimately hindering performance. Recently, Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) pre-trained on large-scale datasets have achieved a significant generalization ability on downstream tasks, yet their potential for UDASS remains largely unexplored. Thus, in this work, we rethink and explore the potential of VFMs in UDASS and propose a framework via VFMs for UDASS. Specifically, we first construct a general VFM-based UDASS framework by combining different VFMs with various decoders, and then evaluate its effectiveness on a standard UDASS benchmark under both full-parameter and frozen-parameter fine-tuning paradigms. Then, we propose a generalized parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach containing a Dual Distribution Alignment (DDA) strategy and a Selective Feature Refinement (SFR) strategy. The DDA strategy aligns covariance differences while enforcing semantic consistency, thereby improving source–target distribution alignment. The SFR strategy employs learnable tokens to refine frozen VFM features based on the source–pseudo-target covariance, preserving domain-invariant information while adapting to target-specific cues. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in multiple UDASS benchmarks. The code is released on https://github.com/seabearlmx/VFM4UDASS.

item.title: SMFormer: Empowering Self-supervised Stereo Matching via Foundation Models and Data Augmentation
item.dc:creator: Yun Wang
Zhengjie Yang
Jiahao Zheng
Zhanjie Zhang
Dapeng Oliver Wu
Yulan Guo
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Recent self-supervised stereo matching methods have made significant progress. They typically rely on the photometric consistency assumption, which presumes corresponding points across views share the same appearance. However, this assumption could be compromised by real-world disturbances, resulting in invalid supervisory signals and a significant accuracy gap compared to supervised methods. To address this issue, we propose SMFormer, a framework integrating more reliable self-supervision guided by the Vision Foundation Model (VFM) and data augmentation. We first incorporate the VFM with the Feature Pyramid Network (FPN), providing a discriminative and robust feature representation against disturbance in various scenarios. We then devise an effective data augmentation mechanism that ensures robustness to various transformations. The data augmentation mechanism explicitly enforces consistency between learned features and those influenced by illumination variations. Additionally, it regularizes the output consistency between disparity predictions of strong augmented samples and those generated from standard samples. Experiments on multiple mainstream benchmarks demonstrate that our SMFormer achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance among self-supervised methods and even competes on par with supervised ones. Remarkably, in the challenging Booster benchmark, SMFormer even outperforms some SOTA supervised methods, such as CFNet. Published: 2026-04-27T19:52:08+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yun Wang; Zhengjie Yang; Jiahao Zheng; Zhanjie Zhang; Dapeng Oliver Wu; Yulan Guo

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684415

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

Recent self-supervised stereo matching methods have made significant progress. They typically rely on the photometric consistency assumption, which presumes corresponding points across views share the same appearance. However, this assumption could be compromised by real-world disturbances, resulting in invalid supervisory signals and a significant accuracy gap compared to supervised methods. To address this issue, we propose SMFormer, a framework integrating more reliable self-supervision guided by the Vision Foundation Model (VFM) and data augmentation. We first incorporate the VFM with the Feature Pyramid Network (FPN), providing a discriminative and robust feature representation against disturbance in various scenarios. We then devise an effective data augmentation mechanism that ensures robustness to various transformations. The data augmentation mechanism explicitly enforces consistency between learned features and those influenced by illumination variations. Additionally, it regularizes the output consistency between disparity predictions of strong augmented samples and those generated from standard samples. Experiments on multiple mainstream benchmarks demonstrate that our SMFormer achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance among self-supervised methods and even competes on par with supervised ones. Remarkably, in the challenging Booster benchmark, SMFormer even outperforms some SOTA supervised methods, such as CFNet.

item.title: Progressive decision refinement for high-resolution multi-focus image fusion
item.dc:creator: Wenyi Zheng
Mingqiang Zhou
Di Xiao
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Existing deep learning-based multi-focus image fusion solutions are typically designed and implemented at obsolete low resolutions. Nowadays, the increasing resolution of modern imaging sensors makes real-world applications more challenging due to the large train-test resolution discrepancy and the massive computational consumption at high resolutions. To address these problems, this paper proposes a progressive decision refinement method for high-resolution multi-focus image fusion, termed PDRFuse. In the methodology, we replace the traditional focus-property detection with coarse decision refinement, which is insensitive to resolution variations. Decision refinement is applied progressively across multiple resolution levels to bridge the resolution gap between training and testing, thereby enabling high-resolution image fusion even with low-resolution training. To effectively manage high-resolution inputs while minimizing computational consumption, we devise a decision refinement network that integrates structural re-parameterization with the state-space model. The network decouples the training and testing processes through re-parameterization and uses the fusion state-space model to fuse valuable features with linear complexity, ensuring better compatibility in high-resolution applications. In the experiment, we develop a high-resolution test dataset to give more persuasive evaluations of the proposed method and other prospective works. Both qualitative and quantitative analyses demonstrate that our method yields high-quality fusion results, outperforming fifteen state-of-the-art methods in fusion performance and efficiency, particularly in high-resolution scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/zwy0913/PDRFuse Published: 2026-04-28T07:09:00+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.801 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wenyi Zheng; Mingqiang Zhou; Di Xiao

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104434

Relevance: 0.801 (must_read)

Existing deep learning-based multi-focus image fusion solutions are typically designed and implemented at obsolete low resolutions. Nowadays, the increasing resolution of modern imaging sensors makes real-world applications more challenging due to the large train-test resolution discrepancy and the massive computational consumption at high resolutions. To address these problems, this paper proposes a progressive decision refinement method for high-resolution multi-focus image fusion, termed PDRFuse. In the methodology, we replace the traditional focus-property detection with coarse decision refinement, which is insensitive to resolution variations. Decision refinement is applied progressively across multiple resolution levels to bridge the resolution gap between training and testing, thereby enabling high-resolution image fusion even with low-resolution training. To effectively manage high-resolution inputs while minimizing computational consumption, we devise a decision refinement network that integrates structural re-parameterization with the state-space model. The network decouples the training and testing processes through re-parameterization and uses the fusion state-space model to fuse valuable features with linear complexity, ensuring better compatibility in high-resolution applications. In the experiment, we develop a high-resolution test dataset to give more persuasive evaluations of the proposed method and other prospective works. Both qualitative and quantitative analyses demonstrate that our method yields high-quality fusion results, outperforming fifteen state-of-the-art methods in fusion performance and efficiency, particularly in high-resolution scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/zwy0913/PDRFuse

item.title: FLAIR-HUB: Large-scale multimodal dataset for land cover and crop mapping
item.dc:creator: Anatol Garioud
Sébastien Giordano
Nicolas David
Nicolas Gonthier
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: The growing availability of high-quality Earth observation (EO) data enables accurate global land cover and crop type monitoring. However, the volume and heterogeneity of these datasets pose major processing and annotation challenges. To address this, the French National Institute of Geographical and Forest Information (IGN) is actively exploring how to exploit diverse EO data, which require large annotated datasets. IGN introduces FLAIR-HUB, a novel large-scale multi-sensor land cover dataset with very-high-resolution (20cm) annotations, covering 2528km 2 " role="presentation"> 2 2 of France. This dataset uniquely integrates six complementary and aligned modalities, enabling more accurate and fine-grained land cover description: aerial imagery, optical multi-spectral Sentinel-2 and SAR Sentinel-1 satellite time series, high-resolution SPOT satellite images, topographic data, and historical aerial images. Extensive benchmarks evaluate multimodal fusion and deep learning models (convolutional neural networks, transformers) for land cover or crop mapping and also explore multi-task learning. Results underscore the complexity of multimodal fusion and fine-grained classification, with best land cover performance (78.2% accuracy, 65.8% mean Intersection over Union) achieved using nearly all modalities. FLAIR-HUB provides a valuable foundation for advancing supervised and pretraining approaches in Earth Observation research, with data and code openly available. Published: 2026-04-27T21:58:36+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.799 (must_read)
